explanation: TRUE POSITIVE. The bug is confirmed. `_getInstRevs` at line 290 of LibraryIR.sol accepts `uint[] memory indexes`, while callers at lines 260 and 272 pass `emap.idxName[instName]` and `emap.idxDate[earnDate]`, which are `uint[] storage` arrays (from `mapping(bytes32 => uint[])` and `mapping(uint => uint[])`). Solidity copies the entire storage array to memory before the function executes, making gas cost O(N) for N total indexed entries regardless of the requested `count` or `iBegin` pagination parameters. This completely defeats the library's stated pagination design ("Caller must page" comments throughout). The contrast with the correctly implemented "get all results" branch (lines 275-286) which directly iterates over `InstRev[] storage values` confirms the author intended but failed to apply storage-based access in `_getInstRevs`. While `getInstRevs` is external view (typically called off-chain with generous gas), the function's pagination guarantees are fundamentally broken and will cause escalating gas waste as datasets grow. Severity of "minor" is appropriate given the view-only nature and the large dataset sizes required for actual out-of-gas DoS.
